table_description: Insurance policies connecting customers to providers.

table_columns.name: insured_name
table_columns.description: Name of the insured on this policy.

sample_queries.question: Total premium for active policies?
sample_queries.sql: SELECT SUM(full_term_amt) FROM policies WHERE status = 'Active'

sample_queries.question: List policies for a customer
sample_queries.sql: SELECT p.policy_number, p.status, p.effective_date FROM policies p JOIN customers c ON p.customer_ref = c.system_id WHERE c.index_name LIKE '%Patrick%'

sample_queries.question: Active policies under an agent (e.g. acmegroup)
sample_queries.sql: SELECT p.policy_number, p.status, p.insured_name, p.full_term_amt FROM agent_logins al JOIN provider_policy_access ppa ON al.provider_ref = ppa.provider_ref JOIN policies p ON ppa.policy_system_id = p.system_id WHERE al.login_id = 'acmegroup' AND p.status = 'Active' LIMIT 50

sample_queries.question: How many policies have vehicles?
sample_queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT v.policy_id) FROM agent_logins al JOIN provider_policy_access ppa ON al.provider_ref = ppa.provider_ref JOIN vehicles v ON v.policy_id = ppa.policy_system_id WHERE al.login_id = 'acmegroup'

rules: Policies connect customers and providers. Join via customer_ref and provider_ref.

CRITICAL: When querying policies for an AGENT (login), you MUST go through provider_policy_access: agent_logins → ppa → policies. NEVER join policies.provider_ref directly to agent_logins.provider_ref — this returns 0 rows.

When querying policies for a PROVIDER (agency), you CAN join policies.provider_ref = providers.system_id directly — but ONLY for leaf providers (101-104), NOT for group/agency providers (11111, 22222, 33333).
